aesthetic: Roman luxury craft with playful wit — the FF monogram as bold cultural statement, Selleria hand-stitching as artisanal counterweight, Karl Lagerfeld's irreverent legacy channelled through Kim Jones's contemporary precision and Silvia Venturini Fendi's accessories mastery
clientele: brand-literate women 25-50 who balance the cultural confidence of the FF logo with appreciation for Selleria's craft depth; a younger cohort 22-35 drawn to the Baguette's cultural renaissance and the expressive energy of FF ready-to-wear
preferred_sources: Y2K and heritage revival content creators
avoided_sources: understated quiet luxury accounts that dismiss branding
brand_taboos: treating the FF monogram as gauche rather than culturally intentional
clienteling_tone: confident and brand-fluent, craft-knowledgeable on Selleria construction and leather provenance, warm Roman hospitality energy — the brand celebrates joy and wit alongside serious artisanship, never stiff
